title: Pancreatic-targeted lipid nanoparticles based on organ capsule filtration
author: Jiaqi Lei
fullText:
Data availabilityAll data generated and supporting the findings of this study are available within the Article and its Supplementary Information. The raw sequencing data reported in this study have been deposited at the Genome Sequence Archive under accession number PRJCA051840. Data for VLDLR expression across various organs in humans are accessible in the DISCO36. Data on PD-L1 expression in pancreatic tumours and its correlation with survival are available in the GEPIA37. Source data are provided with this paper.ReferencesGBD 2021 Diabetes Collaborators. J.L., X.D., K.Y., S.Q. and W.C. conducted the experiments. J.L. and X.D. co-proposed the role of organ capsules in pancreatic-targeted delivery. J.L., W.Z., Y.W. and X.D. contributed to chemical synthesis. J.L., H.L. and X.L. contributed to the construction of CAR T cells. W.C. and X.S. collected the clinical samples and performed the PET–CT experiment. J.L., K.Y., S.Q., Y.W., B.B., Y.F., J.G. and Y.L. contributed to investigation.  Additional informationPublisher’s note Springer Nature remains neutral with regard to jurisdictional claims in published maps and institutional affiliations.Extended data figures and tablesExtended Data Fig. 1 A systematic illustration of pancreatic targeted mechanism.The AH-LNP undergoes four states to realize pancreatic-targeted delivery through CAMP and VMP mechanisms. The pancreatic-targeted capability based on CAMP is feasible across materials and species. The durable pancreatic genome editing and pancreatic cancer therapy can be achieved by using AH-LNP as the delivery vehicle. The diagram was created using BioRender. Lei, J. (2025) https://BioRender.com/e27l63p; https://BioRender.com/9zpe11l.Extended Data Fig. 2 Across-species thickness measurements of organ superficial membrane structure.a, H&E staining of abdominal major organs from human, macaque, pig and mouse. The diagram was created using BioRender. Lei, J. (2025) https://BioRender.com/e27l63p; https://BioRender.com/9zpe11l. b, Quantitative measurements of the superficial membrane structure of abdominal major organs (n = 3 technical replicates). The measured thickness refers to the serosal layer for the intestine, and capsule and muscular layer for the spleen according to physiological structures. Data are presented as mean ± s.d.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 3 Size-dependent CAMP mechanism.a, Ex vivo bioluminescence imaging of the mice injected with ALC-0315-LNP@mRNALuci formulations with different sizes at 6 h post-injection. b–d, Zeta potential (b), RNA integrity number (c) and mRNA encapsulation efficiency (d) of ALC-0315-LNP@mRNALuci formulations with different sizes (n = 5 independent experiments). Data are presented as mean ± s.d. e, Representative CLSM images of the intestine, stomach, and kidney from Ai14 reporter mice injected with ALC-0315-LNP@mRNACre 300 nm in diameter. tdTom is shown in red. f, Rapid accumulation of ALC-0315-LNP@DiR formulations with different sizes in different organs. g, Relative FI of liver to pancreas of the mice injected with ALC-0315-LNP@mRNALuci formulations with different sizes at 6 h post-injection (n = 3 biologically independent replicates). Data are presented as mean ± s.d. h, Morphology analysis of Liposomes@DiR, Polymersomes@DiR, PLA-NPs@DiR and SiO2-NPs@DiR by Cryo-TEM and SEM. The images are representative of n = 3 independent experiments. i,j, Representative ex vivo fluorescence imaging of major organs (i) and relative FI of the pancreas to liver (j) from mice injected with Liposomes@DiR, Polymersomes@DiR, PLA-NPs@DiR and SiO2-NPs@DiR of different sizes (n = 5 biologically independent replicates). Data are presented as mean ± s.d. Unpaired, two-tailed t-tests were used.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 4 Chemical structures of AA-lipids.Chemical structures of lipid backbones, single AA-modified lipids (AA1–AA39-lipids), AA-lipids varying in N-terminal modification, arginine protection, linkage and lipid tails (AA40–AA45-lipids), and dipeptide-modified AA-lipids (AA46–AA65-lipids).Extended Data Fig. 5 Pancreatic targeted performance of AA-LNPs.a, Relative expression efficiency in the pancreas of mice injected with D-type AA-LNP@mRNALuci or L-type AA-LNP@mRNALuci formulations. Note: AA10 is an achiral amino acid. b,c, Chemical structures of AA40–AA45-lipids (b) and ex vivo imaging of major organs from the mice injected with the corresponding AA-LNP@mRNALuci formulations (c). d, Relative DiR FI of the suspensions of pancreatic cells from the mice injected with AA-LNP@DiR formulations.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 6 Chemical structures of AA-lipids.Chemical structures of AA-lipids varying in arginine positioning and conjugation (AA66–AA125-lipids).Extended Data Fig. 7 Size enlargement of AH-LNP and pancreatic targeted delivery study.a, b, DLS results of ALC-0315-LNP@mRNALuci and AH-LNP@mRNALuci in PBS (a) and peritoneal lavage fluid (b). Data are presented as mean ± s.d. (n = 3 independent experiments) for (a). c, TEM images of ALC-0315-LNP and AH-LNP before and after the incubation with peritoneal lavage fluid. Scale bar, 100 nm. The images are representative of n = 3 independent experiments. d,e, PET-CT imaging of the macaques administered with 89Zr-ALC-0315-LNP (d) and 89Zr-AH-LNP (e) formulations at 6 h post-injection. f, Mean SUV of the major organs from macaques injected with 89Zr-ALC-0315-LNP and 89Zr-AH-LNP formulations at 6 h post-injection (n = 3 biologically independent replicates). Data are presented as mean ± s.d. Ordinary two-way ANOVA with Šídák’s multiple comparisons test was used. g, CLSM images of the VLDLR expression in the pancreas from Ai14 reporter mice injected with AH-LNP@mRNACre. The images are representative of n = 3 independent experiments. h, UMAP visualizations of the pancreas defined by scRNA-seq.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 8 Gene editing using AH-LNP as vehicle.a, Chemical structures of lipids composing DOTAP-LNP. b, Size change of DOTAP-LNP@mRNALuci in peritoneal lavage fluid. c, Heat map of the fold change in the adsorbed proteins on DOTAP-LNP@mRNALuci after the incubation with peritoneal lavage fluid. d, A schematic illustration of the experimental procedure used to evaluate the efficiency of pancreatic gene editing in NOD/Ltj mice. The diagram was created using BioRender. Lei, J. (2025) https://BioRender.com/9zpe11l. e, Quantitative analysis of MHC I expression in the pancreas from the mice after various treatments at different time (n = 3 biologically independent samples). Data are presented as mean ± s.d. f,g, Statistical results (f) and representative FCM result (g) of the frequency of CD3+IFN-γ+ T cell in the pancreas of the mice after various treatments (n = 5 biologically independent samples). Data are presented as mean ± s.d. Ordinary one-way ANOVA with Tukey’s multiple-comparison test was used. I: PBS, II, DOTAP-LNP@mRNACas9@sgRNAH2−D1b,H2−K1d, III: AH-LNP@mRNACas9@sgRNAH2−D1b,H2−K1d. h, Distribution of the mRNALuci-positive cells in the pancreas from the mice injected with AH-LNP@mRNALuci. i, Trajectories identified within the stromal cell subpopulations. j, Distribution of the mRNALuci-positive MSCs, fibroblasts and stellate cells transfected by AH-LNP@mRNALuci. k, A schematic illustration of durable gene editing by AH-LNP. l, Distribution of the mRNALuci-positive cells in the pancreas from mice injected with DOTAP-LNP@mRNALuci (n = 11606 single cells). m, Trajectories identified within the macrophage and monocyte subpopulations. n, Distribution of the mRNALuci-positive monocytes and macrophages transfected by DOTAP-LNP@mRNALuci. o, A schematic illustration of ephemeral gene editing by DOTAP-LNP.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 9 Antitumour therapy using AH-LNP as vehicle.a, IL-2 levels in major organs from the mice injected with AH-LNP@mRNAIL-2 and ALC-0315-LNP@mRNAIL-2 at 6 h post-injection (n = 6 biologically independent replicates). b,c, Time-dependent IL-2 expression in liver (b) and pancreas (c) from the mice injected with AH-LNP@mRNAIL-2 and ALC-0315-LNP@mRNAIL-2 (n = 3 biologically independent replicates). Data are presented as mean ± s.d. for (a–c). Se, serum; H, heart; Li, liver; Sp, spleen; Lu, lung; Ki, kidney; Pa, pancreas; In, intestine. d, Pancreatic selectivity of AH-LNP@mRNAIL-2 and ALC-0315-LNP@mRNAIL-2 (n = 6 biologically independent replicates). e, Therapeutic schedule of mice bearing Pan02KRAS tumour. f, FCM results of intratumoural CD3+CD8+CD45+ T cells, GZM-B+CD8+ T cells and IFN-γ+CD8+ T cells from the mice injected with PBS, ALC-0315-LNP@mRNAIL-2 or AH-LNP@mRNAIL-2 (n = 3 biologically independent replicates). g, Radiance measurements of Pan02KRAS tumour-bearing mice after various treatments (n = 3 biologically independent replicates). h, FCM results of the immune responses in Pan02KRAS tumour-bearing mice after various treatments (n = 3 biologically independent replicates). I, PBS; II, Vax-mRNAKRAS; III, Vax-mRNAKRAS + ALC-0315-LNP@mRNAIL-2; IV, Vax-mRNAKRAS + AH-LNP@mRNAIL-2. i, FCM results of immune responses in mice after various treatments (n = 3 biologically independent replicates). i, PBS; iii, CAR T + ALC-0315-LNP@mRNAanti-PD-1×IL-2; iv, CAR T + AH-LNP@mRNAanti-PD-1×IL-2. j, Ex vivo lysis ability of total T cells from mice treated with CAR T + ALC-0315-LNP@mRNAanti-PD-1×IL-2 and CAR T + AH-LNP@mRNAanti-PD-1×IL-2 on day 60 (n = 3 biologically independent samples). k, A schematic illustration of CAR T cell therapy for pancreatic cancer using AH-LNP@ mRNAanti-PD-1×IL-2. The diagram was created using BioRender. Lei, J. (2025) https://BioRender.com/9zpe11l. Unpaired, two-tailed t-tests were used in (d). Ordinary one-way ANOVA with Tukey’s or Dunnett’s multiple-comparison test was used in (f,h,i). Data are presented as mean ± s.d.Source dataExtended Data Fig. 10 Universality study and safety evaluation.a–c, In vivo and ex vivo imaging of the mice administered with DiR-labelled LNP@mRNALuci formulations derived from Py-lipid (a), Q-N-lipid (b), and Imi-lipid (c). d, A schematic illustration of the universality study using four-, three- and two-component LNPs. The diagram was created using BioRender. Lei, J. (2025) https://BioRender.com/y0t1vn6. e,f, Representative in vivo and ex vivo imaging and organ biodistribution of the luminescence and fluorescence signals of the mice administered with DiR-labelled LNP@mRNALuci formulations derived from AA76-lipid/cholesterol/PEG-lipid LNP (e) and AA76-lipid/DSPC/PEG-lipid LNP (f). g–i, LNP composition, representative in vivo and ex vivo imaging and organ biodistribution of the luminescence and fluorescence signals of the mice administered with DiR-labelled LNP@mRNALuci formulations derived from AA76-lipid/cholesterol (g), AA76-lipid/DSPC (h) and AA76-lipid/PEG-lipid two-component LNPs (i). j, H&E staining of the pancreas and liver from the mice injected with PBS and AH-LNP. Scale bar, 100 μm. The images are representative of n = 3 independent experiments.Source dataSupplementary informationSource dataRights and permissionsSpringer Nature or its licensor (e.g. a society or other partner) holds exclusive rights to this article under a publishing agreement with the author(s) or other rightsholder(s); author self-archiving of the accepted manuscript version of this article is solely governed by the terms of such publishing agreement and applicable law.Reprints and permissionsAbout this articleCite this articleLei, J., Yang, K., Cao, W. et al.
